1.texto_original: OR Davide venne in Nob, al Sacerdote Ahimelec; ed Ahimelec fu spaventato del suo incontro, e gli disse: Perchè sei tu solo, e non v’è alcuno teco?
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 21:1

"OR Davide venne in Nob, al Sacerdote Ahimelec; ed Ahimelec fu spaventato del suo incontro, e gli disse: Perchè sei tu solo, e non v’è alcuno teco?"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 21:1

Philological Analysis

"OR Davide venne in Nob, al Sacerdote Ahimelec; ed Ahimelec fu spaventato del suo incontro, e gli disse: Perchè sei tu solo, e non v’è alcuno teco?"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"OR Davide venne in Nob, al Sacerdote Ahimelec; ed Ahimelec fu spaventato del suo incontro, e gli disse: Perchè sei tu solo, e non v’è alcuno teco?"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

2.texto_original: E Davide disse al Sacerdote Ahimelec: Il re m’ha comandato qualche cosa, e m’ha detto: Niuno sappia nulla di ciò perchè io ti mando, e di ciò che t’ho ordinato. E, quant’è a’ miei fanti, io li ho assegnati a trovarsi in un certo luogo.

5.texto_original: E Davide rispose al Sacerdote, e gli disse: Anzi le donne sono state appartate da noi dall’altro ieri che io partii; e gli arnesi de’ fanti già erano santi; benchè il nostro viaggio sia per affare che non è sacro; quanto più adunque sarà oggi quel pane tenuto santamente fra i nostri arnesi?

9.texto_original: E il Sacerdote rispose: Io ho la spada di Goliat Filisteo, il qual tu percotesti nella valle di Ela; ecco, ella è involta in un drappo dietro all’Efod; se tu te la vuoi pigliare, pigliala; perciocchè qui non ve n’è alcuna altra, se non quella. E Davide disse: Non ve n’è alcuna pari; dammela.

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
13.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Ed egli si contraffece in lor presenza, e s’infinse pazzo fra le lor mani; e segnava gli usci della porta, e si scombavava la barba."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: Ed Achis disse a’ suoi servitori: Ecco, voi vedete un uomo insensato; perchè me l’avete voi menato?
14.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 21:14

"Ed Achis disse a’ suoi servitori: Ecco, voi vedete un uomo insensato; perchè me l’avete voi menato?"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 21:14

Philological Analysis

"Ed Achis disse a’ suoi servitori: Ecco, voi vedete un uomo insensato; perchè me l’avete voi menato?"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.
